title: Registry-as-context-cache POC
outcome: E04.S5 closure — registry-as-context-cache POC verified end-to-end after S1–S4 audit fixes. Re-ran the epic §S5 checklist: wfos+ds descriptors present (S1); ontarch validate gate PASS (S1); units.json generated from colocated descriptors via ontarch sync (S2); Workstreams/.agents/ four subdirs + READMEs on disk and committed in Workstreams repo (S3); no-agent-git-push policy present (S4); token-cost POC proof consolidated in this session record. Audit pass also landed doc drift fixes (metadata-plane graphs implemented, graphs/README example corrected, agent-rails publish-rails section, AGENTS.md enforcement boundary), optional unit→uses→unit graph edges in ontarch_emit_graph, and wfos E04 S1–S4 commit (7c1936c).
validation: S5 checklist — [x] wfos+ds descriptors (3 total incl. panoply); [x] ontarch validate task (moon run ontarch:validate PASS: 3 descriptors + 2 policies + graph 14 nodes/11 edges); [x] units.json from colocated descriptors (ontarch sync: 3 units); [x] .agents/{profiles,tools,skills,graphs} + READMEs (5 READMEs on disk); [x] no-agent-git-push policy; [x] token-cost note (see token_note). Gates: panoply:doctor → tools.json (44 tools); ontarch:sync → registry + local-toolkit.yml; ontarch:validate PASS. jq spot-checks: kind=workspace → ds,wfos; layer=application → ds,wfos; requires proto → wfos.
decisions: S5 is verification + recording — checklist items were delivered across S1–S4; this session proves the POC end-to-end and closes the epic formally

token-cost POC: one wfos.descriptor.toml read (or one jq filter over units.json) replaces scanning moon.yml + packages/*/moon.yml + READMEs + .prototools to infer kind/layer/paths/entrypoints/capabilities/policy
no-agent-git-push remains Ontarch policy metadata at Level 0 — documented enforcement boundary matches secret-tools pattern until Takogami routes commands
uses edges added to graph emitter when requires∩provides across units (none today because capability namespaces do not overlap between units)
token_note: POC proof: to act on wfos, an agent reads one descriptor or one filtered registry query (e.g. jq -f queries/by-kind.jq). Without the registry that is a dozen-file scan — root moon.yml, each packages/*/moon.yml, package READMEs, .prototools, and AGENTS.md — to infer the same kind/layer/paths/entrypoints/capabilities/policy the descriptor states directly. The registry is generated, compact, and gitignored; schemas/descriptors/policies stay tracked. One read vs N-file scan is the M1 token lever.
